target_cmd: for i in $(seq 1 30); do curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' https://www.solaceagi.com/api/v1/health; done | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5
target_name: Hulk Smash — 30 Rapid Requests to /health (Rate Limiting Test)
context: Hulk Smash oracle (q-agi-055): What breaks under stress? Send 30 rapid requests to /api/v1/health. Health endpoint should handle burst traffic. Expected: mostly 200s (health check shouldn't be rate-limited). If we see 429s on health, rate limiting is too aggressive. If we see 503s, the service crashes under load. Count response codes: a healthy service returns 200 for all 30. The Hulk tests what the polite user never would.
